body:
## Summary

Fixes #242 by routing malformed English meaning questions such as `what i digress mean?` through the existing concept/Wikipedia/Wikidata/Wiktionary lookup chain instead of the unknown fallback.

## What Changed

- Added general meaning-question parsing in Rust and the browser worker for forms like `what i X mean`, `what do X mean`, `what is X meaning`, and `what is the meaning of X`.
- Added regression coverage for the exact issue prompt, broader meaning-question variants, and supported-language dictionary fallback prompts.
- Added Cambridge Dictionary, Merriam-Webster, Dictionary.com, Collins, and Wiktionary to source/connectivity records as appropriate.
- Kept non-CORS dictionary pages out of the live default browser fusion plan while exposing them in connectivity diagnostics for page/proxy checks.
- Rebuilt `src/web/formal_ai_worker.wasm` after the shared web-search registry change.
- Added the issue case study and raw artifacts under `docs/case-studies/issue-242/`.

## Reproduction

Before the fix:

- `cargo test issue_242_definition_prompt_typo_extracts_dictionary_term -- --nocapture` failed because `extract_concept_query("what i digress mean?")` returned `None`.
- `npx playwright test tests/issue-242.spec.js --config playwright.local.config.js` failed with the unknown-prompt response.

After the fix, the browser test forces Wikipedia and Wikidata misses and verifies the prompt reaches Wiktionary with the extracted search term `digress`.

## Verification

Pre-merge:

- `src/web/wasm-worker/build.sh`
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `cargo test` (484 passed, 69 ignored)
- `npm run test:local` from `tests/e2e` (193 passed)

Post-merge with current `origin/main`:

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `cargo test` (484 passed, 69 ignored)
- `npm run check:language-test-coverage`
- `npm run check:i18n`
- `npm run check:language-parity`
- `npm run check:intent-coverage`
- `npm run test:local` from `tests/e2e` (194 passed)

## Notes

- The file-size check passes with pre-existing warning annotations for near-limit files; no hard limit violations.
- Cambridge Dictionary returned Cloudflare 520 to a direct HEAD request in this environment, so it is intentionally treated as a page/proxy connectivity source rather than a direct CORS API provider.
- The first post-merge CI run failed the language test coverage gate; commit `7a6c177` adds the required `en`, `ru`, `hi`, and `zh` prompt coverage.

title: Fix malformed meaning prompts and dictionary sources
